sutta: on a certain occasion monks i myself was staying at uru vela on the bank of the river naranjara under the goat hurts banyan just after i had become perfectly enlightened then monks a great number of brahmins broken down all men aged far gone in years who had reached life's end came to visit me where i was on reaching me they greeted me courteously and after the exchange of greetings and courtesy sat down at 1 side as they thus said monks those brahmins said this to me we have heard it said master gautama that gotama the recluse pays no respect to does not rise up in the presence of does not offer a seat to brahmins who are broken down all men aged far gone in years who have reached life's end in as much master gautama as the worthy gautama does none of these things it is not the proper thing to do master gotama 10 monks i thought to myself in truth these reverent ones understand not either the elder or the things which make an elder do a man be old monks 80 or 90 or 100 years of age yet if he be 1 who speaks out of due season who speaks things untrue and unprofitable things contrary to dhamma doctrine and contrary to vinaya discipline if he be 1 who utters words unworthy to be treasured in the heart words unseasonable and devoid of reason words undiscriminating and not concerned with welfare then that 1 is reckoned just a foolish elder though a man be young monks a youth a mere land [Music] blackhead and blessed with his lucky prime 1 in the first flush of life if he be 1 who speaks in due season who speaks things true and profitable things according to dhamma and vinaya if he be 1 who utters words worthy to be treasured in the heart words seasonable reasonable discriminating and concerned with welfare then that 1 is reckoned a wise elder now monks there are these 4 things which make the elder what for hearing among is virtuous perfect in the patimoka precepts restrained with the restraint of the patimoka perfect in the practice of right behavior seeing danger in the slightest faults he undertakes and trains himself in the training of the precepts he is learned is replete with learning is a heart of learning those doctrines which lovely at the beginning lovely in the middle lovely at the end both in the meaning and the letter of them which preach the utterly fulfilled the the perfectly purified way of the holy life such doctrines are much heard by him born in mind repeated aloud pondered over and well penetrated by his vision the 4 stages of ghana sometimes translated as translated as mental absorption as i prefer like states of mental incandescence the 4 stages of jannah which are of the clear mind which are concerned with a happy life in this very world these he wins easily without effort by the destruction of the asavas in this very life thoroughly understanding the release by mind the release by wisdom he realizes it attains it and dwells therein these monks are the 4 things which make the elder and this we find the buddha is trying to define what is an elder what is an elder elder is 1 who is worthy of respect is considered worthy of respect and so the buddha said it's not it's not because a man is old that he's worthy of respect if he speaks words which are not profitable which are not valuable which are untrue etc and uh he's just a foolish old man but uh the buddha said 4 things make an elder like 4 things make among worthy of respect the first 1 is virtua that monk is virtuous that's the first 1 and the second 1 he has much learning is very much learning that means learning of the dhamma the teachings and the third 1 he has attained the 4 jhanas 4 stages of concentration and the last 1 he has destroyed the earth of us that means he has gone out of the cycle of rebirth he has become an arahant so these are the 4 things the first 1 you find sila is always very important sila is moral conduct and it is the foundation of the holy life of the spiritual path of a virtuous man moral conduct without moral conduct you can't progress and there are some foolish people now who think they want to practice only wisdom and they think all the other things like keeping the precepts doing charity learning the dhamma are not important but you cannot make any progress and without all these supporting conditions foreign mentions all the different ways of attaining blessings ashila is 1 of them dhana is another much learning bahu sacha is another visiting amongst who know the dhamma to discuss the dhamma and studying the dharma and discussion of dharma et cetera all these are foundations now of the spiritual path with which aid us to progress and you see the second factor mentioned here is much learning much learning the buddha's disciples are called sabakas savakas means hearers or listeners and they listen to the dhamma all of them that's why listening to the dhamma is extremely important there's a lot of people they try to practice the holy life without this important foundation and they may practice for 20 30 years but they don't make much progress and people see that it's obvious to to other people but it's not obvious to them they have a big temper very egoistic etc so much learning of the dhamma is very important that's why you you find that the buddha's disciples that were enlightened some of them are much faster than the buddha they came and listened to the dhamma and was taught by the buddha in a very short while they were enlightened compared to the buddha who took 6 years of hard work to become enlightened and the fourth 1 is the 4 jaanas the 4 jaanas if a person attains a jana is worthy of respect that's why the buddha said in some other suta that if a monk can attain the jana even for a short while then the food that he eats which are given by other people now he eats uh he's worthy of the food that he takes and etc and also he follows the the buddha's advice now he listens to the buddha's advice the last 1 of course is the the highest criterion now he has destroyed the arts of us he has no more ego just destroyed the ego completely that is a person who sees all beings as he's 1 with all beings and he can never harm other beings so those are the qualities that which make a person an elder worthy of respect and thanks to ties
